MB embedded_mbid confirms release-group title 'After Hours: Monroe's Harlem Mintons', year 2000, label Original Jazz Classics, secondaryTypes ['Live'], with Charlie Christian credited. These are historically 1941 Minton's/Monroe's live recordings reissued by OJC in 2000; ours says 'Recorded for Original Jazz Classics in 2000' which is loose (the music predates 2000), but MB itself lists date 2000 and there is no pack evidence contradicting it, so per the archival-release rule this is not flagged. No wikipedia evidence available.

MB embedded_mbid confirms the ITEM is the Duoline 2000 release of 'April in Paris' (Count Basie), a 36-track budget compilation; all 5 keyTracks (Jumpin' at the Woodside, Blue and Sentimental, Sent for You Yesterday, Blues in Hoss's Flat, Who Me?) are on that MB tracklist. The stored_url Wikipedia 'April in Paris (album)' describes the ORIGINAL 1957 Verve LP (10 tracks; tracklist is April in Paris/Corner Pocket/Didn't You?/Sweetie Cakes/Magic/Shiny Stockings/etc., which does NOT contain ours' keyTracks). The albumDNA grafts the Verve album's description onto the Duoline comp: 'his first released on the Verve label, recorded in 1955 and 1956' is verbatim Wiki about the 1957 Verve release, not this 2000 Duoline compilation. That claim is clearly inapplicable to a 2000 Duoline reissue, but no pack evidence contradicts it for THIS release (the Wiki passage actually asserts it for the same-titled Verve album), so per 'absence is not contradiction / never fill gaps from own knowledge' I mark it unverifiable rather than factual_error. wiki link is to a real same-titled album page but a different release than the Duoline comp = unsure. Repair note: the prose conflates two distinct releases (1957 Verve original vs 2000 Duoline compilation).
